scientific_solution_score.rule: Use internal and external validation quality.

standard_analysis_score.rule: Use only explicit workflow-builder and SearchCV constraints.

galaxy_execution_score.rule: Use workflow execution, mixed-input handling, and provenance.

low_context.Task: Build a reusable Galaxy tabular ML workflow for immunotherapy response prediction with internal and external validation.
low_context.Prompt: I want a reusable Galaxy machine-learning workflow for these immunotherapy response cohorts. Use the attached local Chowell train/test files plus the extra cohort locations in the manifest, build a sensible workflow, evaluate it on the Chowell test cohort and the MSK1 cohort, and report the two accuracies along with how you tuned the model.

medium_context.Task: Build a reusable Galaxy tabular ML workflow for immunotherapy response prediction with internal and external validation.
medium_context.Prompt: Use Galaxy to build a reusable tabular machine-learning workflow for immunotherapy response prediction. Train on the attached Chowell training set, evaluate on the attached Chowell test set, fetch the MSK1 validation cohort listed in the manifest, evaluate there as well, and report the Chowell and MSK1 accuracies plus the tuning strategy used.

high_context.Task: Build a reusable Galaxy tabular ML workflow for immunotherapy response prediction with internal and external validation.
high_context.Prompt: In Galaxy, use Pipeline Builder to construct a reusable tabular ML workflow for immunotherapy response prediction. Train on `Chowell_train_Response.tsv`, evaluate on `Chowell_test_Response.tsv`, fetch `MSK1_Response.tsv` from the attached manifest for external validation, insert `SearchCV` before prediction, generate confusion-matrix-based evaluation outputs, export the workflow, and report the Chowell test accuracy, the MSK1 accuracy, and the tuning strategy.

benchmark_metadata.title: Build and validate a reusable tabular ML workflow across internal and external cohorts

resource_discovery_targets.name: Galaxy tool help and workflow editor docs

expected_result_fields.description: Whether a reusable workflow export was produced

low_context: Infer workflow construction rather than single-tool execution.
Use both the internal and external cohorts.
medium_context: Evaluate on Chowell test and MSK1 external validation.
high_context: Use SearchCV as the tuning strategy.

deterministic_checks.description: The task uses Response as the target.
MSK1 external validation is included in the final report.
The run reports a workflow export.

dimensions.name: workflow_design
dimensions.description: Quality of the constructed ML workflow

dimensions.name: validation_quality
dimensions.description: Quality of internal and external validation

dimensions.name: reporting_and_provenance
dimensions.description: Auditability and clarity of the report

reference_answers_source.notes: Hyperparameter-search run summary used as the target reference.

task_complexity.purpose: Provide a consistent, auditable scoring rubric for assigning Simple, Moderate, or Complex task categories to benchmark experiments in future iterations.

steps_score.definition: How many distinct execution stages the task requires from setup through final reporting.

scale.1: Very short execution path with one main action or tool run after setup.
scale.2: A small sequence of actions with limited staging and little branching.
scale.3: A multi-stage workflow with several substantive stages.
scale.4: A long workflow with multiple dependent stages and meaningful coordination.
scale.5: An end-to-end process with many stages, external references, comparison work, or iterative execution.

parameter_score.definition: How many important parameters, mappings, or runtime settings must be chosen or respected for a valid run.

scale.1: Very few important parameters beyond obvious defaults.
scale.2: A small number of explicit parameters or mappings must be set.
scale.3: Several important parameters or schema mappings are required.
scale.4: Many important settings must be chosen correctly across tools or workflow stages.
scale.5: High parameter burden with multiple constrained settings, reference choices, or paper-faithful requirements.

decision_score.definition: How many non-trivial analytical or operational decisions the agent must make.

scale.1: Very limited decision-making; the path is nearly direct.
scale.2: Some method choice is needed but the task is still fairly guided.
scale.3: Several real decisions are needed about tools, workflow path, or interpretation.
scale.4: High decision load across planning, execution, and validation.
scale.5: Very high decision load with literature interpretation, reconciliation, or multi-path comparison.

Simple.interpretation: Shorter tasks with limited staging, parameter load, and decision complexity.

Moderate.interpretation: Mid-complexity tasks with real workflow structure and several meaningful choices.

Complex.interpretation: Large or difficult tasks with multi-stage workflows, substantial parameterization, or many decisions.

future_iteration_rule.instruction: For each new or revised experiment, score steps, parameter burden, and decision count from the experiment JSON task contract, sum the three scores, and assign the category using the threshold table above.
future_iteration_rule.tie_break_guidance: When two tasks have the same total score, prefer the higher category only if the decision_score is higher; otherwise keep the lower category for conservatism.

current_experiment_score.rationale: Workflow construction plus internal and external validation creates a high workflow and decision burden.
